budget_wines.name: Altano Douro Tinto
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Widely stocked in Portuguese supermarkets and wine shops; exported through Symington's distributors across Europe, the UK and the US.
budget_wines.description: The Symington family's everyday Douro red, a fruity unoaked blend of traditional grapes. Reliable, juicy and one of the easiest serious-producer entry points into the valley for well under ten euros.
budget_wines.tip: The unoaked Altano tinto drinks young and bright; the Altano Reserva steps up with French-oak ageing if you want more for a few euros more.

budget_wines.name: Niepoort Fabelhaft Tinto
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Stocked across Portuguese and German wine retailers and Niepoort's export partners; widely available online from around ten euros.
budget_wines.description: Niepoort's fresh, fragrant everyday Douro red from organically farmed fruit. Light on its feet with peppery red-fruit lift, it punches well above its price and is a benchmark value bottle of the valley.
budget_wines.tip: Look for the standard Fabelhaft for everyday drinking; the Fabelhaft Reserva adds oak and depth at a still-fair price.

budget_wines.name: Crasto Douro Tinto
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Sold at Quinta do Crasto in Gouvinhas and through the estate's distributors in Portugal, the UK and the US.
budget_wines.description: Quinta do Crasto's fresh, fruity entry red built from the classic Douro field-blend grapes. Ripe red fruit, gentle structure and easy charm from one of the valley's most respected estates.
budget_wines.tip: It is the gateway to the Crasto range; trade up to the Crasto Reserva Old Vines when you want the estate's serious side.

budget_wines.name: Crasto Douro Branco
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available at Quinta do Crasto and through the estate's Portuguese and export retailers.
budget_wines.description: A crisp, citrus-and-stone-fruit Douro white from indigenous high-elevation grapes. Fresh, mineral and a fine demonstration that the valley's whites deserve attention, all for well under fifteen euros.
budget_wines.tip: Serve well chilled with grilled fish; Douro whites are an under-the-radar value category compared with the famous reds.

budget_wines.name: Quinta do Vallado Douro Tinto
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Sold at Quinta do Vallado near Peso da Regua and through its distributors in Portugal, the UK and the US.
budget_wines.description: Vallado's flagship estate red, deep ruby with ripe black cherry and plum over gentle spice. A polished, modern Douro blend from one of the founding Douro Boys, dependable year after year.
budget_wines.tip: A strong choice for first-timers wanting the modern Douro style; the Vallado Reserva and single-varietal bottlings climb from here.

budget_wines.name: Ramos Pinto Duas Quintas Tinto
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available at Ramos Pinto in Vila Nova de Gaia and through the house's international distributors.
budget_wines.description: A smooth, finely rich Douro red from two Ramos Pinto estates, layering ripe blackberry with subtle oak and ample acidity. A consistently rewarding bottle from a historic port house's table-wine range.
budget_wines.tip: It has the structure to take a few years in the cellar but drinks happily now with grilled meat.

budget_wines.name: Altano Douro Branco
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Stocked in Portuguese supermarkets and wine shops, and via Symington's export distributors.
budget_wines.description: The white sibling of Symington's Altano range, a fresh, citrus-driven Douro white from indigenous grapes. Clean, gently aromatic and an easy everyday value bottle under ten euros.
budget_wines.tip: Serve very cold as an aperitif or with shellfish; it is one of the cheapest ways to taste the Douro's native white grapes.

budget_wines.name: Ramos Pinto Ruby Port
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Sold at Ramos Pinto's Gaia lodge and through the house's distributors internationally.
budget_wines.description: A fresh, fruity entry Ruby Port from the historic Ramos Pinto house. Bright berry sweetness and a warming finish make it an affordable introduction to fortified Douro wine.
budget_wines.tip: Ruby is the youngest, fruitiest style of Port; chill it lightly and pour over chocolate desserts or simply as a sweet nightcap.

budget_wines.name: Graham's Fine Tawny Port
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available at Graham's 1890 Lodge in Vila Nova de Gaia and through Symington's worldwide distributors.
budget_wines.description: Graham's entry tawny, wood-aged for a soft, nutty, caramel-edged style. An accessible everyday tawny from one of the great vintage-Port houses, ideal for those starting with fortified wine.
budget_wines.tip: Serve lightly chilled with almonds or a dried-fruit dessert; tawny keeps for weeks after opening, unlike vintage Port.

budget_wines.name: Niepoort Drink Me Nat Cool Tinto
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Sold through natural-wine importers and Portuguese retailers, typically in a one-litre bottle.
budget_wines.description: Niepoort's fun, glouglou Nat Cool red, made for chilling and easy drinking in litre format. Low-intervention, bright and juicy, it is the playful, gulpable face of the Douro for around a tenner.
budget_wines.tip: Serve it lightly chilled; the litre bottle is built for sharing and the style is deliberately fresh and uncomplicated.
